brief_notes: Solid methods paper presenting TEMPy-REFF for cryo-EM structure refinement using Gaussian mixture models with B-factor refinement and ensemble generation. Large-scale benchmark on 366 maps is a strength, with statistically significant improvements over deposited and CERES models (mean CCC improvement of 0.197 and 0.150 respectively). B-factor convergence robustness is well-demonstrated. Map segmentation and composition applications are practically useful. Case studies with RNA Pol III, nucleosome-CHD4, and SARS-CoV-2 polymerase provide concrete demonstrations. AlphaFold2 integration is timely. Mathematical formulation is clear. Tables with quantitative data are well-structured. Minor weaknesses: speed benchmarks could be more comprehensive; force field comparison shows minimal difference, raising questions about sensitivity. Discussion could address limitations more explicitly.
